element_introduction_order.first_appears: Normal Game mode - rounds 7-10 (after defeating 6 new characters)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players already familiar with these from prequel; reintroduced as proven threats in tournament
Introduces player to new movesets and fighting statistics as tournament progresses
Fundamental mechanic available immediately; optional taunt also mapped to same button
New defensive mechanic; learned through encounter with projectile-based opponents
Advanced defensive option; discovered through experimentation or opponent pressure
First boss encounter; represents skill checkpoint before final boss
Ultimate challenge; requires mastery of all mechanics and character knowledge

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Selectable after Normal Game completion or from menu
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Environmental hazards and seesaw lifebar add complexity; alternative ruleset for experienced players
Unique mechanic changing victory conditions; requires button-mashing recovery mechanic understanding

typical_relief_triggers: Health bar recovery between rounds (best-of-three structure)
typical_tension_triggers: Low health state (seesaw lifebar danger zone in Death Match)
Timer running down with close health matchup
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive tournament escalation with difficulty curve rising from round 1-6 (new characters) through rounds 7-10 (returning characters) to bosses. Best-of-three round structure creates micro-tension cycles within each match.

difficulty_curve.early_game: 6 new characters introduce players to balanced roster and new mechanics (throw, taunt, projectile reflection, counter-grab) in relatively controlled tournament environment
difficulty_curve.mid_game: 8 returning characters from World Heroes 1 randomly selected; players must adapt to established movesets and greater opponent variety. Increased opponent aggression expected
difficulty_curve.late_game: Two boss encounters (Neo Geegus semi-final, Dio final) represent skill gates requiring mastery of all mechanics and defensive options learned throughout tournament
notable_spikes: Transition from new to returning characters (rounds 6-7): sudden increase in opponent experience and predictability
Neo Geegus encounter: first boss with enhanced abilities
Dio final boss: ultimate difficulty spike with full movepool mastery required
Death Match mode: seesaw lifebar and environmental hazards significantly elevate complexity

notable_level_patterns.description: Normal Game mode uses fixed sequence: 6 new characters (introduction phase) → 4 randomly-selected original characters (adaptation phase) → 2 bosses (skill gate phase). Creates predictable difficulty progression with controlled variable in middle section.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: World Heroes 2 Normal Game mode structure

notable_level_patterns.description: Each opponent requires winning 2-of-3 rounds, creating mini-tension cycles within matches. Victory conditions based on knockout or health comparison if timer expires. Encourages adaptation between rounds.

Death Match mode introduces stage-specific environmental hazards and seesaw lifebar mechanic, creating secondary difficulty branch. Transforms match victory conditions and requires different strategic approach than Normal Game.
